game_info.title: EXCEPTION - AI Programming Puzzle
game_info.concept: **异常;** "异常" is a single-player puzzle game where players program the AI of robotic units to complete level objectives. In each sequential level, players interact with a visual programming interface, dragging and dropping command blocks or inputting code snippets to define robot behaviors such as "move forward 3 units," "attack nearest enemy," or "wait 2 seconds." After programming, players tap a "Run Simulation" button to observe their robots execute the AI, with success determined by eliminating all enemies or reaching a designated exit point. Progression involves unlocking new levels that introduce additional robot types, environmental hazards, and increasingly complex enemy patterns, requiring players to craft sophisticated AI programs for multiple units to cooperate. Difficulty scales by increasing the number of robots to manage, the intricacy of required AI logic, and the precision needed in command sequencing, with failure occurring if any robot is destroyed or the objective is not met.
game_info.description: EXCEPTION is a puzzle programming game where you act as a system administrator debugging a corrupted sector. Your task is to program autonomous "Thread Units" to navigate treacherous grids, avoid data corruption spikes, and neutralize bugs. Instead of direct control, you pre-plan a sequence of commands (Move, Turn, Attack, Wait) for your units. Once your logic is set, execute the simulation and watch your code run. Success requires precise logic and spatial reasoning to clear all objectives without losing a unit.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Left/Right: Navigate through the command timeline slots
- Arrow Up/Down: Cycle through available commands (Move, Turn Left, Turn Right, Attack, Wait)
- Space: Clear the command in the current slot
- Shift: Switch between active robots (when multiple are available)
- Z: Run Simulation / Stop Simulation (Edit Mode)
- Enter: Start Game / Proceed to Next Level
- R: Restart current level
- ESC: Pause / Resume

TEST_1.test_description: Verify that the programming interface correctly registers a command and the simulation executes it.
TEST_1.strategy_description: In Level 1, the test will select the first command slot, assign a "MOVE_FORWARD" command using Arrow Keys, and press 'Z' to run the simulation.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The robot's grid position should change from its starting coordinates in the direction it is facing after the simulation tick.

TEST_1.expected: The robot's grid position should change from its starting coordinates in the direction it is facing after the simulation tick.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify level completion logic by solving the first level.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test will input the exact sequence of commands required to walk the robot from the start to the exit tile in Level 1, then execute the simulation.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The robot reaches the goal tile, and the game state transitions to a "LEVEL_COMPLETE" or "WIN" state, unlocking the next level.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Program the robot to move directly into a wall tile and execute the simulation.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The robot should remain at its position and not pass through the wall tile.

TEST_4.test_description: Verify fail state when robot is destroyed.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Program the robot to move into a hazard/spike tile.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The game state should transition to "GAME_OVER_LOSE" upon the robot entering the hazard.
TEST_4.description: Verify fail state when robot is destroyed.

TEST_5.strategy_description: In a multi-unit level, press Shift to toggle active unit selection.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: The `gameState.activeUnitIndex` should update, and the UI should reflect the command queue of the newly selected unit.
